title: aquilegia alpina subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Aquilegia alpina subsp. pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean rock bellflower, is a subspecies of the alpine columbine plant, which belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Aquilegia alpina subsp. pyrenaica include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease congestion, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
